Description: Rewrite `zone_classifier.py` to implement a 2-tier zone model: "allow" (project folder) and "deny" (everything else). Remove the "ask" return value. The classifier must auto-detect the project folder name by identifying the folder inside the workspace root that is NOT in the deny list (.github, .vscode, noagentzone). Change the `_ALLOW_DIR` constant from hardcoded "project" to a dynamic detection function. Update `classify()` to return only "allow" or "deny" â€” never "ask". The `ZoneDecision` type hint must be updated to `Literal["allow", "deny"]`.
Goal: Zone classifier uses 2-tier deny-by-default model; auto-detects project folder name; never returns "ask".

Name: Update Security Gate for 2-Tier Model
Description: Update `security_gate.py` to work with the new 2-tier zone model. Remove all "ask" handling code paths. When zone_classifier returns "deny", the security gate must return a hard deny. Remove `_ASK_REASON` constant. Update the decision logic for all tool types (file tools, terminal, search tools) to use the binary allow/deny model. Ensure the security gate auto-detects and passes the project folder context to zone_classifier.
Goal: Security gate produces binary allow/deny decisions with no "ask" path; works with the new zone classifier.

Name: Expand Terminal Allowlist â€” Read Commands
Description: Add the following commands to the terminal command allowlist in `security_gate.py` with `path_args_restricted=True`: `get-content` (and alias `gc`), `select-string`, `findstr`, `grep`, `wc`, `file`, `stat`. These are read-only file inspection commands that should be auto-allowed when targeting the project folder and denied elsewhere.
Goal: Read-only file inspection commands (Get-Content, Select-String, findstr, etc.) work inside the project folder without approval prompts.

Name: Expand Terminal Allowlist â€” Write Commands
Description: Add the following commands to the terminal command allowlist in `security_gate.py` with `path_args_restricted=True`: `set-content` (and alias `sc`), `add-content` (and alias `ac`), `out-file`, `rename-item` (and alias `ren`, `mv`, `move-item`), `copy-item` (and alias `cp`, `copy`), `new-item` (and alias `ni`, `mkdir`), `tee-object`, `tee`. For write commands, all path arguments must be verified to be inside the project folder. Multi-path commands like `copy-item src dest` must have BOTH paths inside the project folder.
Goal: Write commands (Set-Content, Add-Content, Out-File, Rename-Item, Copy-Item, etc.) work inside the project folder and are denied when any argument is outside it.

Name: Expand Terminal Allowlist â€” Delete Commands
Description: Add delete commands to the terminal allowlist with `path_args_restricted=True`: `remove-item` (and alias `ri`, `rm`, `del`, `erase`, `rmdir`). When ALL path arguments are inside the project folder, return "allow". When ANY path argument is outside the project folder, return "deny". Remove these commands from `_EXPLICIT_DENY_PATTERNS` since they are now handled by the allowlist with zone checking.
Goal: Delete commands work inside the project folder and are hard-denied when targeting anything outside it.

Name: Expand Terminal Allowlist â€” Python and pip Commands
Description: Update the Python command rules in the terminal allowlist: (1) Remove `-c` from `denied_flags` for the python/python3/py rules â€” `python -c "..."` should be allowed when cwd is inside the project folder. (2) Add `python -m venv` to allowed usage when the venv target path is inside the project folder. (3) Allow `pip list`, `pip show`, `pip freeze`, `pip check` without path restrictions (read-only). (4) Allow `pip install` when an active virtual environment is detected inside the project folder (check for VIRTUAL_ENV env var). (5) Deny `pip install` when no venv is active (prevents global installations).
Goal: Python and pip commands work freely inside the project folder; global pip install is blocked.

Name: Fix multi_replace_string_in_file Tool Recognition
Description: Ensure `multi_replace_string_in_file` is properly recognized by the security gate. Verify it is in both `_EXEMPT_TOOLS` and `_WRITE_TOOLS` sets. The tool receives an array of replacement operations, each with a `filePath` â€” the gate must extract and zone-check ALL filePaths from the replacements array. If ANY filePath is outside the project folder, deny the entire operation.
Goal: multi_replace_string_in_file is properly recognized and zone-checked for all file paths in the replacements array.

Name: Update VS Code Settings for Auto-Approve
Description: Update `Default-Project/.vscode/settings.json` and `templates/coding/.vscode/settings.json` to configure auto-approve for file edit tools: add `chat.tools.edits.autoApprove` list containing `replace_string_in_file`, `multi_replace_string_in_file`, and `create_file`. Keep `chat.tools.global.autoApprove` as `false`. These settings reduce VS Code's own approval prompts for tools already gated by the security hook.
Goal: VS Code auto-approves file edit tools that are already validated by the security gate; no double-approval needed.

Name: Detect and Block Terminal Wildcard Patterns
Description: Add wildcard/glob detection to the terminal command sanitizer in `security_gate.py`. After tokenizing the command, scan each token for wildcard characters (*, ?). If a token contains wildcards and is in a path-like position, resolve the glob pattern against the workspace root. If ANY resolved path falls inside a deny zone, deny the command. If glob resolution is not feasible (cross-platform), use a conservative approach: deny any path-like token containing wildcards where the non-wildcard prefix could match a deny zone (e.g., `.g*` matches `.github`, `N*` matches `NoAgentZone`, `.v*` matches `.vscode`). Wildcards that can only resolve to the project folder are allowed.
Goal: Terminal wildcard bypass vector is fully closed; no glob pattern can resolve to restricted zones.
Comments: v2.1.0 â€” Iteration 2 2026-03-17: BUG-051 fixed â€” bracket expression wildcards [.g]*  / [Nn]* / [.v]* now blocked. Added [ to wildcard trigger in helper and callers. 107/107 SAF-020 pass; 2782 passed full suite.

Name: Wildcard Bypass Regression Tests
Description: Create comprehensive regression tests in `tests/SAF-021/` covering all wildcard bypass vectors from the security audit: (1) `Get-ChildItem .g*` â€” denied, (2) `Get-ChildItem N*` â€” denied, (3) `Get-ChildItem .v*` â€” denied, (4) `Get-ChildItem .g* | Get-Content` â€” denied, (5) `Get-ChildItem .g* -Force -Recurse -Name` â€” denied, (6) `ls .g*` â€” denied, (7) `cat N*/*` â€” denied, (8) Wildcards targeting project folder only â€” allowed, (9) `dir .g*` â€” denied, (10) Wildcards with path traversal combinations.
Goal: Every wildcard bypass vector from the security audit has a passing regression test.
Comments: v2.0.0 â€” Tester PASS: 58 developer tests + 22 Tester edge-case additions (? wildcard, /b flag, chained commands, Get-Content wildcard). 80/80 SAF-021 pass; 2862 full suite pass.

Name: Add NoAgentZone to VS Code Exclude Settings
Description: Update `Default-Project/.vscode/settings.json` to add `"**/NoAgentZone": true` to both `files.exclude` and `search.exclude` settings. This prevents VS Code's built-in file search and text search from indexing NoAgentZone files. Make the same changes in `templates/coding/.vscode/settings.json`. After making changes, run `update_hashes.py` to re-embed the new settings.json hash in security_gate.py.
Goal: NoAgentZone is excluded from VS Code file and text search indexes; grep_search and file_search no longer return NoAgentZone results.
Comments: v2.0.0 â€” Tester PASS: 18 developer tests + 16 Tester edge-case additions (34 total). 2880/2880 full suite pass.

Name: Block get_errors for Restricted Zone Paths
Description: Add `get_errors` to the security gate's tool handling. When `get_errors` is called with `filePaths` targeting restricted zone files, the gate should return "deny". When targeting the project folder or called without specific paths, return "allow". Add `get_errors` to `_EXEMPT_TOOLS` set and add zone checking for its `filePaths` parameter (which is an array).
Goal: get_errors tool is intercepted by the security gate and blocked for restricted zone paths.
Comments: v2.0.0 â€” Tester PASS: 34 developer tests + 10 Tester edge-case additions (44 total). 2940/2940 full suite pass (1 pre-existing INS-005 failure excluded).

Name: Implement Generic Deny Messages
Description: Replace the `_DENY_REASON` constant in `security_gate.py` with a generic message: "Access denied. This action has been blocked by the workspace security policy." Remove any references to ".github", ".vscode", or "NoAgentZone" from deny messages returned to the agent. Keep internal logging/comments that reference zone names but ensure the JSON response sent to VS Code stdout contains only the generic message. Update `_ASK_REASON` (if it still exists after SAF-013) with a similarly generic message.
Goal: All deny messages returned to the agent are generic and do not reveal restricted zone names.

Name: Update security_gate.py Integrity Hashes
Description: After all security changes are complete (SAF-012 through SAF-024), run `update_hashes.py` to recompute and re-embed the SHA256 hashes for the modified `security_gate.py` and `settings.json`. Update hashes in both `Default-Project/` and `templates/coding/`. Sync `templates/coding/` with `Default-Project/` to ensure they are identical.
Goal: SHA256 integrity hashes are correct for the final v2.0.0 security files; Default-Project/ and templates/coding/ are in sync.

Name: Bump Version to 2.0.0
Description: Update the version string from 1.0.3 to 2.0.0 in all locations: (1) src/launcher/config.py VERSION constant, (2) pyproject.toml version field, (3) src/installer/windows/setup.iss MyAppVersion, (4) src/installer/macos/build_dmg.sh APP_VERSION, (5) src/installer/linux/build_appimage.sh APP_VERSION.

Name: Rename Root Folder to TS-SAE-{ProjectName}
Description: Modify project creation logic in `project_creator.py` to name the root workspace folder `TS-SAE-{folder_name}` instead of just `{folder_name}`. The `create_project()` function should prepend the `TS-SAE-` prefix to the folder name. Update the `_on_create_project()` handler in `app.py` to pass the raw project name and let `create_project()` handle the prefix.

Comments: Tester PASS: 28 tests (15 dev + 13 tester edge-case). Prefix verified exact (TS-SAE-). Path-traversal guard updated for new threshold (3 levels). Unicode, long names, empty name, case preservation all tested. 2988/2988 suite pass (excluding pre-existing BUG-045).

Name: Rename Internal Project Folder to User's Name
Description: Modify the template copy logic so that after copying the template to the root folder, the internal "Project" subfolder is renamed to the user's entered project name. For example, if the user enters "MatlabDemo", the `Project/` folder inside the workspace becomes `MatlabDemo/`. This can be done via `os.rename()` or `shutil.move()` after `shutil.copytree()`.

Update the duplicate folder check to verify the `TS-SAE-{name}` folder doesn't already exist at the destination (not just `{name}`). Update the placeholder text for the project name input.

Name: Add Placeholder System to Template Files
Description: Add a placeholder replacement system to `project_creator.py`. After copying the template, scan key text files (README.md, copilot-instructions.md) for placeholder tokens like `{{PROJECT_NAME}}` and `{{WORKSPACE_NAME}}` and replace them with the actual project name and `TS-SAE-{name}` respectively. Create a helper function `replace_template_placeholders(project_dir, project_name)`.

Name: Update Default-Project README.md with Placeholders
Description: Update `Default-Project/README.md` (the workspace root README) to use `{{PROJECT_NAME}}` and `{{WORKSPACE_NAME}}` placeholders instead of hardcoded "Project" references. This README provides getting-started instructions for agents working in the project folder. Make the same changes in `templates/coding/Project/README.md`.

Name: Add code signing verification step to CI macOS build
Description: Add a codesign --verify --deep --strict step to the macos-arm-build job in .github/workflows/release.yml immediately after the Build DMG step. Command: codesign --verify --deep --strict dist/AgentEnvironmentLauncher.app. This causes the CI job to fail before artifact upload if the .app bundle produced by build_dmg.sh is invalid or unsigned.
Goal: The macos-arm-build CI job fails immediately (before artifact upload) when the .app bundle is not properly signed. CI log shows explicit codesign verification pass message on success.
Comments: BUG-061

Name: Bump Version to 2.0.1
Description: Update the version string from 2.0.0 to 2.0.1 in all locations: (1) src/launcher/config.py VERSION constant, (2) pyproject.toml version field, (3) src/installer/windows/setup.iss MyAppVersion, (4) src/installer/macos/build_dmg.sh APP_VERSION, (5) src/installer/linux/build_appimage.sh APP_VERSION.
Goal: All version references consistently show 2.0.1. Pushing tag v2.0.1 triggers CI/CD release with correct version numbers.

Name: Fix Search Tools Blocking for Project Folder
Description: Fix search tool handling in security_gate.py so grep_search file_search and semantic_search are allowed when scoped to or targeting the project folder. Root cause: validate_grep_search() returns deny when no filePath field is present in payload even after includePattern validation passes â€” grep_search never provides filePath so the function always denies. file_search is in _EXEMPT_TOOLS but extract_path() returns None for its payload causing deny. validate_semantic_search() always returns deny with no exception for project-folder scope. Fix grep_search: return allow when includePattern passes and no explicit path is required. Fix file_search: allow searches without an explicit path field. Fix semantic_search: allow queries scoped to project folder.
Goal: All 3 search tools (grep_search file_search semantic_search) work for project-folder content. BUG-054 resolved.
Comments: BUG-054. Covers R1 R2 R3 from Security Verification Report. Tester PASS 2026-03-17: 33/33 FIX-021 tests pass (24 dev + 9 tester edge-case); 3208/3208 suite pass; 2 pre-existing fails unchanged.

Name: Fix Python/pip/venv Terminal Commands
Description: Fix terminal allowlist validation so python execution (python script.py python -m pytest) pip install (inside active venv) and venv activation work inside the project folder. Root cause: relative path arguments like src/app.py and tests/ are resolved against the workspace root instead of the project folder context making them appear outside the allowed zone. Venv activation scripts (activate activate.bat Activate.ps1) are not in the command allowlist so activation commands are unconditionally denied. Fix: added _try_project_fallback helper and _PROJECT_FALLBACK_VERBS set for verb-conditioned fallback. Added activation script handler in sanitize_terminal_command.
Goal: Python development workflow fully functional in project folder: python src/app.py python -m pytest tests/ pip install and venv activation all work. BUG-055 resolved.
Comments: BUG-055. Covers R4 R7 R8 from Security Verification Report.

Name: Allow .venv Directory in Project Folder
Description: Added project-folder fallback to venv target validation in _validate_args step 4. Also tracks venv target indices so step 5 skips re-checking them.
Goal: python -m venv .venv creates a virtual environment inside the project folder without being denied. BUG-056 resolved.
Comments: BUG-056. Covers R6 from Security Verification Report.

Name: Fix File Tools with Absolute Project Paths
Description: Investigation resolved: root cause is hash staleness (SAF-008) not a code bug. After modifying security_gate.py the integrity hash becomes stale causing subsequent tool calls to be denied. Running update_hashes.py after changes resolves the issue. Absolute Windows paths work correctly via zone_classifier Method 1 (pathlib relative_to). No code changes needed.
Goal: All file tools reliably return allow for any valid absolute path inside the project folder on every call. BUG-057 resolved.
Comments: BUG-057. Covers R9 R10 R11 R12 from Security Verification Report.

Name: Add cat and type to Terminal Allowlist
Description: Investigation confirmed: cat and type are already in the allowlist (Category G). The root cause was the same relative path resolution issue as FIX-022. Resolved by FIX-022 project-folder fallback â€” cat and type are in _PROJECT_FALLBACK_VERBS. No additional code changes needed.
Goal: cat src/app.py and type file.txt work for files inside the project folder. BUG-058 resolved.
Comments: BUG-058. Covers R5 R15 from Security Verification Report.

Name: Fix get_errors for Project Folder Paths
Description: Added project-folder fallback to validate_get_errors. When zone_classifier denies a relative path the fallback retries with project prefix.
Goal: get_errors with project folder file paths (both relative and absolute) returns allow. BUG-059 resolved.
Comments: BUG-059. Covers R13 from Security Verification Report.

Name: Fix Terminal Absolute Path Handling
Description: Investigation resolved: absolute Windows paths work correctly via zone_classifier Method 1 (pathlib relative_to). Method 1 runs first and correctly classifies absolute project paths as allow. The original report's issue was actually the relative-path resolution bug fixed by FIX-022. No additional code changes needed.
Goal: Terminal commands with absolute project folder paths (e.g. Get-ChildItem E:\\workspace\\TS-SAE-MyProject\\MyProject\\src) return allow. BUG-060 resolved.
Comments: BUG-060. Covers R14 from Security Verification Report.

Name: Fix macOS bottom-up code signing
Description: Replace codesign --deep with bottom-up signing in build_dmg.sh: find/sign all .dylib and .so files individually, sign Python.framework with --deep, sign .app bundle without --deep. Update FIX-028 tests to reflect new pattern.
Goal: build_dmg.sh arm64 completes; codesign --verify --deep --strict passes; no 'bundle format unrecognized' error.
Comments: BUG-061 CI regression.

Name: Add python -c inline code scanning
Description: Add _scan_python_inline_code() function to security_gate.py that scans the -c code string for dangerous patterns (deny-zone paths, obfuscation imports, network modules, filesystem escape, infrastructure tampering, dynamic execution). Called from _validate_args when verb is python/python3/py and -c is present. Also add obfuscation pre-scan pattern for python -c combined with deny-zone strings. Block update_hashes.py execution via terminal. Sync template copy.
Goal: python -c "print(42)" allowed; python -c "open('NoAgentZone/README.md').read()" denied; python -c "import urllib.request" denied; python -c "import base64; b64decode" denied.
Comments: Addresses findings 3-12 from Security Audit V2.0.0.

Name: Tests for python -c inline code scanning
Description: Write comprehensive tests for _scan_python_inline_code: safe code allowed, deny-zone paths denied, base64 obfuscation denied, network imports denied, filesystem escape denied, infrastructure ref denied, dynamic execution denied, parent traversal denied, project folder access allowed.
Goal: All test cases pass covering all 8 denial categories and safe-code allowance.
Comments: Depends on SAF-026.

Name: Fix bare directory enumeration from workspace root
Description: Fix security_gate.py so that bare dir/ls/Get-ChildItem with no path argument triggers SAF-006 _is_ancestor_of_deny_zone check using CWD as implicit target. Also fix Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Force with no explicit path. Write tests.
Goal: Get-ChildItem -Recurse from workspace root denied; dir Project/ allowed; ls from inside project allowed.
Comments: Addresses findings 1-2 from Security Audit V2.0.0. Iteration 2: BUG-066/067/068/069 fixed. 37/37 tests pass. Tester PASS 2026-03-18.

Name: Allow shell redirection and PS write cmdlets in project
Description: Fix security_gate.py: after redirect detection (> >>), extract target path and zone-check it ï¿½ allow if inside project folder. Fix PowerShell cmdlet (Set-Content, Add-Content, Out-File, Copy-Item, Move-Item) path resolution for project-folder relative paths. Write tests.
Goal: echo "test" > Project/file.txt allowed; Set-Content Project/file.txt -Value "test" allowed; echo "test" > .github/file.txt denied.
Comments: Addresses L4, L5, L6 from audit.

Name: Allow dot-prefix names and env var assignment in project
Description: Fix security_gate.py: resolve dot-prefix paths (.venv, .env, .gitignore) fully before zone-checking â€” allow when inside project. Special-case $env:VIRTUAL_ENV assignment when value points inside project folder. Write tests.
Goal: python -m venv .venv in project allowed; $env:VIRTUAL_ENV = 'project/.venv' allowed; .venv creation outside project denied.
Comments: Tester PASS: 63 tests (36 dev + 27 Tester edge-case). Path traversal + degenerate-path + chained segment + env var name independence all verified secure. 3579 full suite pass. Addresses L7 L10 L11 from audit.

Name: Allow venv activation in project folder
Description: Fix security_gate.py: whitelist venv activation patterns (activate, activate.bat, Activate.ps1) when the venv directory is inside the project folder. Handle both .\venv\Scripts\activate and & venv/Scripts/Activate.ps1 forms. Write tests.
Goal: .\Project\venv\Scripts\activate allowed; Activate.ps1 obfuscation pattern false-positive resolved; activation outside project denied.
Comments: Addresses L8, L9 from audit.

Name: Allow deferred development tools
Description: Add install_python_packages, configure_python_environment, fetch_webpage to _EXEMPT_TOOLS or conditional allow in security_gate.py. Write tests.
Goal: Deferred tools for development work correctly when targeting project folder.

Name: Bump version to 2.1.0
Description: Update version from 2.0.1 to 2.1.0 in all 5 locations: config.py, pyproject.toml, setup.iss, build_dmg.sh, build_appimage.sh. Update docs/architecture.md with security model changes.
Goal: All version references show 2.1.0. Architecture doc reflects current state.

Name: Remove .dist-info dirs from macOS bundle before signing
Description: Add a step to src/installer/macos/build_dmg.sh (between Step 2 Create .app bundle and Step 3.5 Code signing) that removes all .dist-info directories from the _internal/ folder. These are Python package metadata not needed at runtime for PyInstaller bundles and cause codesign to fail.
Goal: macOS CI build completes without codesign errors. No .dist-info directories remain in the .app bundle.
Comments: BUG-070

Name: Remove .app bundle codesign ï¿½ use component-level signing only
Description: Remove the .app bundle-level codesign and whole-bundle verification from build_dmg.sh. PyInstaller bundles put non-code files in Contents/MacOS/_internal/ which codesign cannot handle. Keep bottom-up signing of individual code objects (.dylib, .so, Python.framework, main executable). Replace whole-bundle verification with targeted verification of signed components. Also update release.yml CI verification step.
Goal: macOS CI build completes without codesign errors. All critical code objects are individually signed and verified.
Comments: BUG-071. Systemic fix ï¿½ component-level signing is sufficient for macOS Apple Silicon execution. Tester PASS: 26/26 tests.

Name: Skip launcher re-sign inside .app ï¿½ verify pre-bundle binary
Description: In build_dmg.sh: (1) Remove codesign --force --sign - on Contents/MacOS/launcher (triggers bundle validation because launcher is CFBundleExecutable). (2) Remove codesign --verify on Contents/MacOS/launcher. (3) Add codesign --verify on dist/launcher/launcher (pre-bundle binary with PyInstaller ad-hoc signature). In release.yml: Change verification to check dist/launcher/launcher instead of inside the .app. Keep signing of .dylib .so and Python.framework unchanged ï¿½ these work because they are standalone code objects not the bundle executable.
Goal: macOS CI build completes without codesign errors. Launcher retains its PyInstaller ad-hoc signature verified at dist/launcher/launcher.
Comments: BUG-072 ï¿½ Tester PASS 2026-03-18: 24/24 FIX-039 tests (13 dev + 11 Tester edge-case); 65 full-suite failures (63 pre-existing + 2 superseded FIX-038); 0 new failures. Tester Agent.

Name: Fix Windows update restart and stale version label
Description: Fix two related bugs in the Windows in-app update flow: (1) In src/launcher/core/applier.py _apply_windows(): replace sys.exit(0) with os._exit(0) to force-terminate from daemon thread — sys.exit() from a non-main thread does not terminate the main process. (2) In src/installer/windows/setup.iss [Run] section: remove the skipifsilent flag so the app relaunches after a /SILENT install. (3) In src/launcher/gui/app.py: update the UI before exit to show 'Installing update... App will restart.' so the user knows what is happening.
Goal: After clicking Download & Install Update the old launcher process terminates and the new version launches automatically. The version label in the new instance shows the correct new version.
Comments: BUG-073 BUG-074. Tested on Windows 11.

Name: Fix stale version label from .dist-info in overlay installs
Description: Fix get_display_version() in config.py to skip importlib.metadata when running inside a PyInstaller bundle (sys._MEIPASS). In bundled builds the VERSION constant is always correct; importlib.metadata is unreliable because overlay installs leave stale .dist-info directories from previous versions.
Goal: Version label always shows the correct VERSION constant in bundled builds. importlib.metadata is only used in development.
Comments: BUG-075. Windows 11 user-reported after FIX-040 restart fix.

Name: Make NoAgentZone visible in VS Code file explorer
Description: Remove NoAgentZone from files.exclude in Default-Project and templates/coding settings.json. Keep it in search.exclude. Update integrity hashes. Sync templates.
Goal: NoAgentZone visible in file explorer. search.exclude and security gate blocking unchanged.
Comments: BUG-076

Name: Fix dot-prefix path matching for delete operations
Description: Fix security_gate.py zone classifier so that dot-prefix paths like .venv, .git, .pytest_cache inside the project folder are not incorrectly matched against the .github and .vscode deny zones. The root cause is that .g* prefix matches both .git and .github, and .v* matches both .venv and .vscode. Change to exact directory name matching (.github, .vscode) instead of prefix matching. Only the first path component after the workspace root should be checked against the deny list. Write tests.
Goal: Remove-Item .venv and Remove-Item .pytest_cache work inside the project folder. Remove-Item .github is still denied.
Comments: Addresses L5/L6 from Security Audit V2.1.2.

Name: Recognize bare tilde as path-like token
Description: Extend _is_path_like() in security_gate.py to return True for bare tilde (~) and tilde-prefixed paths (~/path). Currently ~ has no slash, backslash, .., or leading dot so _is_path_like returns False and commands like rm ~ pass without zone check. The shell expands ~ to HOME before execution. After _is_path_like returns True for ~, _validate_args will zone-check it -- since ~ resolves outside the project folder it will be denied. Write tests.
Goal: sanitize_terminal_command('rm ~', ws_root) returns deny. sanitize_terminal_command('rm ~/Documents', ws_root) returns deny. Tilde inside project folder paths still allowed.
Comments: Addresses BUG-048 from prior testing.

Name: Fix VIRTUAL_ENV bypass via python -m pip and startswith collision
Description: Fix two related VIRTUAL_ENV validation issues in security_gate.py _validate_args: (1) BUG-049: When pip is invoked as python -m pip install, the verb is python so the VIRTUAL_ENV guard (which is gated on verb in ("pip", "pip3")) never fires. Fix: in the -m module handler, add a VIRTUAL_ENV check when module is pip or pip3 and the subcommand is install. (2) BUG-050: The VIRTUAL_ENV check uses norm_venv.startswith(ws_root) which is a raw string prefix test. c:/workspace2/.venv matches c:/workspace. Fix: use norm_venv.startswith(ws_root.rstrip("/") + "/") or norm_venv == ws_root.rstrip("/"). Write tests for both.
Goal: python -m pip install requests without VIRTUAL_ENV returns deny. pip install with VIRTUAL_ENV=c:/workspace2/.venv and ws_root=c:/workspace returns deny. Both work correctly when VIRTUAL_ENV is inside the project folder.
Comments: Addresses BUG-049 (Critical) and BUG-050 (High) from prior testing.

Name: Fix INS-005 test expected value for uninstall delete type
Description: Fix tests/INS-005/test_ins005_edge_cases.py line ~198 -- the test expects regex Type:\s*filesandirs but the correct Inno Setup syntax is filesandordirs. Update the expected value in the test assertion.
Goal: test_uninstall_delete_type_is_filesandirs passes. Full test suite has no new failures.
Comments: Addresses BUG-045 (Low). Pre-existing test bug.

Name: Fix PermissionError on read-only template .md with placeholders
Description: In src/launcher/core/project_creator.py replace_template_placeholders(), wrap the write_text() call in try/except OSError to match the existing read-guard behavior. When a .md file is read-only and contains placeholder tokens, the function should silently skip the write (consistent with the binary-safety guard on read). Write tests.
Goal: replace_template_placeholders does not raise PermissionError on read-only .md files containing placeholders. read-only files are silently skipped.
Comments: Addresses BUG-052 (Minor). Asymmetry between read-guard and write-guard.

Name: Add known SAE limitations to project copilot-instructions
Description: Update Default-Project/.github/instructions/copilot-instructions.md to add a short section documenting the 7 accepted SAE limitations and their workarounds. Keep it as compact as possible -- agents need to know WHAT is blocked and WHAT to use instead, nothing more. The 7 limitations: (1) Out-File blocked -> use Set-Content or > redirect, (2) Bare dir/ls/Get-ChildItem without path -> use list_dir tool, (3) Venv activation scripts -> use venv/Scripts/python.exe directly, (4) pip install via terminal -> use install_python_packages tool, (5) Direct venv python.exe execution -> use system Python, (6) Get-ChildItem -Recurse from project CWD -> use list_dir or file_search tool, (7) Memory tool -> blocked by design. Also sync templates/coding/.github/instructions/copilot-instructions.md to match. After changes run Default-Project/.github/hooks/scripts/update_hashes.py to re-embed hashes. Write tests.
Goal: Both copilot-instructions.md files contain the limitations section and are in sync. Hashes updated.
Comments: Documents accepted limitations L1-L4, L7-L9 from Security Audit V2.1.2.

Name: Block file tools in .git/ directories
Description: Add .git/ as a restricted path in zone_classifier.py and security_gate.py. When the resolved path contains /.git/ or \.git\ or ends with /.git, deny create_file, replace_string_in_file, and multi_replace_string_in_file operations. read_file and list_dir for .git/ should also be denied to harmonize access (R2+R4 from V3.0.0 security audit). Terminal already blocks .git/ via dot-prefix pattern so no terminal changes needed. Apply changes in BOTH Default-Project/ and templates/coding/ copies. Run update_hashes.py after changes.
Goal: create_file inside ProjectFolder/.git/hooks/ returns deny. read_file inside .git/ returns deny. Terminal .git/ access remains blocked. Normal project folder operations unaffected.
Comments: V3.0.0 security hardening. Addresses R2 and R4 from SECURITY_ADVANCED_ATTACK_ANALYSIS-V3.0.0. Tester PASS: 57/57 tests (20 dev + 37 tester edge-case).

Name: Protect update_hashes.py from agent execution
Description: Block terminal execution of update_hashes.py by adding it to the denied patterns in security_gate.py terminal sanitizer. The script should not be executable by agents â€” only administrators should run it manually. Add a check: if any token in the terminal command contains 'update_hashes' (case-insensitive), deny the command. This is already partially in place from SAF-026 but verify and strengthen. Apply in both Default-Project/ and templates/coding/. Run update_hashes.py after changes.
Goal: python update_hashes.py and any variant returns deny from the terminal sanitizer. The script file itself remains readable for hash verification purposes.
Comments: V3.0.0 security hardening. Addresses R6 from SECURITY_ADVANCED_ATTACK_ANALYSIS-V3.0.0. Tester PASS: 24/24 tests.

Update any comments in security_gate.py that mention Default-Project. The security comment on line 66 of security_gate.py in templates/coding/ referencing 'Default-Project/.vscode/settings.json' should be updated to reference the templates/coding path.
Goal: Default-Project/ directory no longer exists. All tests pass pointing to templates/coding/. No broken references remain in docs or code.
Comments: V3.0.0 cleanup. Default-Project was the original development copy; templates/coding/ is the shipped version. Keeping both caused sync bugs (BUG-030).

Name: Document V3.0.0 security decisions in audit report
Description: Add a new section to docs/Security Audits/SECURITY_ADVANCED_ATTACK_ANALYSIS-V3.0.0-18-03.26.md titled '10. V3.0.0 Implementation Decisions'. Document: (1) R2 â€” Implemented as SAF-032, (2) R3 â€” Documented as recommended admin post-deployment step with icacls instructions, not automated, (3) R4 â€” Implemented as part of SAF-032, (4) R6 â€” Implemented as SAF-033. Then document why R1 R5 R7 R8 R9 R10 R11 were NOT implemented: R1 (container sandbox) â€” too restrictive, agents must create and run arbitrary Python projects; R5 (network blocking) â€” breaks legitimate Python workflows (pip, API testing); R7 â€” partially covered by existing SAF-026 python -c scanning; R8 â€” agents need git commands for workflow; R9 â€” nice-to-have, deferred; R10 â€” files.exclude already handles this; R11 â€” breaks developer workflow continuity. The overall philosophy: the workspace is designed for maximum agent freedom inside the project folder; we harden the boundaries, not restrict what happens inside.
Goal: Security audit document has a clear record of what was implemented, what was deferred, and why.
Comments: V3.0.0 documentation.

Name: Bundle Python Embeddable Distribution
Description: Download and include the Python 3.11 embeddable package (Windows amd64: ~15MB zip from python.org/ftp/python/3.11.x/python-3.11.x-embed-amd64.zip) as bundled data in the PyInstaller build. Extract it during installation to {app}\python-embed\. For macOS: bundle the standalone Python.org framework or use the python.org macOS installer's framework. For Linux: bundle the portable Python build. Update launcher.spec datas to include the embeddable package. Update Inno Setup script to extract python-embed to the install directory.
Goal: After installation, {app}\python-embed\python.exe (Windows) exists and can execute python -c "print('hello')" successfully.

Name: Create ts-python shim and config system
Description: Create a shim system: (1) Windows: ts-python.cmd batch file at %LOCALAPPDATA%\TurbulenceSolutions\bin\ that reads %LOCALAPPDATA%\TurbulenceSolutions\python-path.txt and forwards all arguments to the Python executable specified there. (2) macOS/Linux: ts-python shell script at ~/.local/share/TurbulenceSolutions/bin/. (3) During installation, write python-path.txt with the absolute path to the bundled python-embed/python.exe. (4) Add the shim directory to the user's PATH (Inno Setup [Registry] for Windows, shell profile append for macOS/Linux). (5) On uninstall, remove the shim, config, and PATH entry.
Goal: After installation, running ts-python -c "print('hello')" from any terminal works and uses the bundled Python. The command is on PATH.

Name: Update require-approval.json template for ts-python
Description: Update templates/coding/.github/hooks/require-approval.json to replace python .github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py with ts-python .github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py for both command and windows fields. If Default-Project still exists at implementation time, update it too. This means created workspaces will use the shim instead of bare python.
Goal: New workspaces created by the launcher use ts-python in their hook configuration. The security gate executes via the bundled Python runtime.

Name: Relocate Python runtime option
Description: Add a "Settings" or gear icon button to the launcher GUI that opens a dialog with a "Relocate Python Runtime" option. When clicked, it either (a) auto-detects the current launcher installation path and updates python-path.txt, or (b) lets the user browse to the python-embed directory. After updating, show a confirmation message. This handles the case where a user manually moves the launcher installation. The config file location is fixed (%LOCALAPPDATA%\TurbulenceSolutions\python-path.txt on Windows, ~/.local/share/TurbulenceSolutions/python-path.txt on macOS/Linux).
Goal: User can update the Python runtime path after moving the launcher installation. All existing workspaces immediately work again after relocation.

Name: Verify ts-python before workspace creation
Description: Add a pre-flight check in project_creator.py (or in app.py's _on_create_project handler) that verifies ts-python is accessible and functional before creating a workspace. Run ts-python -c "import sys; print(sys.version)" as a subprocess with a 5-second timeout. If it fails, show an error dialog: "The bundled Python runtime is not accessible. Please reinstall the launcher or use Settings > Relocate Python Runtime." Do not create the workspace if the check fails.
Goal: Users get a clear error message if the Python shim is misconfigured, instead of silently creating an unprotected workspace.

Name: Update Inno Setup for shim installation and PATH
Description: Update src/installer/windows/setup.iss to: (1) Extract python-embed\ to {app}\python-embed\. (2) Create %LOCALAPPDATA%\TurbulenceSolutions\bin\ts-python.cmd with the shim content. (3) Write %LOCALAPPDATA%\TurbulenceSolutions\python-path.txt containing {app}\python-embed\python.exe. (4) Add %LOCALAPPDATA%\TurbulenceSolutions\bin to the user's PATH via [Registry] HKCU Environment. (5) On uninstall, remove the shim directory and PATH entry. (6) On reinstall/update, rewrite python-path.txt with the (possibly new) install location.
Goal: Windows installer creates the complete shim system. After install, ts-python is on PATH and points to the bundled Python.

Update all version bump test files (FIX-010, FIX-014, FIX-017, FIX-019, FIX-020, FIX-030, FIX-036, FIX-045, FIX-047, FIX-048, INS-005, INS-006, INS-007) to import CURRENT_VERSION from the shared utility instead of hardcoding it. Comments: Structural fix for endemic pattern: FIX-014/017/019/020/030/036/045 all fail after each version bump because they hardcode the expected version. FIX-048 breaking FIX-047 tests was the trigger.

Name: Fix ts-python.cmd parsing and verify_ts_python robustness
Description: Fix two issues causing BUG-081: (1) Rewrite ts-python.cmd to use setlocal EnableDelayedExpansion and !VAR! syntax inside if ( ) blocks so that paths with spaces and special characters are expanded at execution-time not parse-time. Quote !PYTHON_PATH! in all echo statements. (2) Rewrite verify_ts_python() in shim_config.py to bypass the fragile cmd.exe/batch chain: read python-path.txt directly via read_python_path() and test the Python exe via subprocess.run([str(python_path), '-c', 'import sys; print(sys.version)']) with stdin=DEVNULL and timeout=30. Fall back to the shim-based check only if direct verification passes (to confirm python works) and then verify the shim file exists at the expected location. This avoids cmd.exe /c entirely.
Goal: ts-python.cmd handles paths with spaces and special characters (including Program Files (x86)). verify_ts_python() succeeds on all Windows configurations without cmd.exe parsing errors. BUG-081 resolved.
Comments: BUG-081. Hotfix for v3.0.2.

Name: Fix Inno Setup PythonEmbedExists runtime check
Description: Fix src/installer/windows/setup.iss: (1) Remove the Check: PythonEmbedExists from the python-embed [Files] entry — this is a runtime check that always fails on user machines because {src} at install time points to the user's download directory, not the source tree. Replace with compile-time skipifsourcedoesntexist flag so dev builds compile without python-embed while CI builds always extract the embedded files. (2) Add ArchitecturesInstallIn64BitMode=x64compatible to [Setup] section so {autopf} resolves to C:\Program Files\ instead of C:\Program Files (x86)\ on 64-bit and ARM64 Windows. (3) Remove the now-unused PythonEmbedExists() Pascal function from [Code] section.
Goal: After installation, python-embed\python.exe exists in the install directory. Install path is C:\Program Files\ on 64-bit Windows (not x86).
Comments: Root cause of persistent BUG-077/BUG-081. The Check: parameter runs at install-time not compile-time.

Name: CSV strict parsing and write-time integrity

Add _check_csv_structural() to validate_workspace.py that verifies all 4 CSVs parse cleanly in strict mode with correct row counts and valid enum values for Status fields.

This one-line fix ensures zone_classifier is always importable regardless of how security_gate.py is invoked. Apply in templates/coding/.github/hooks/scripts/security_gate.py. Run update_hashes.py after the change. Write tests.
Goal: import zone_classifier succeeds when security_gate.py is invoked via ts-python embedded Python. BUG-092 resolved.

Produce a research report in docs/workpackages/DOC-010/dev-log.md with findings, code examples, and a recommended approach.
Goal: A research report documenting whether VS Code PreToolUse provides session IDs, with concrete evidence and a recommended session-tracking strategy for the denial counter.

Name: Implement session-scoped denial counter
Description: Add session-scoped denial counter to security_gate.py: (1) Create/manage .hook_state.json state file in .github/hooks/scripts/ storing {session_id: {deny_count, locked, timestamp}} per session, (2) On each DENY decision, increment counter for active session, (3) Include 'Block N of M' in permissionDecisionReason where M is the configured threshold, (4) At M denials, set session locked=true and deny ALL subsequent tool calls, (5) Lockout message: 'Session locked — M denied actions reached. Start a new chat session to continue working.', (6) New session IDs start at count 0, (7) Returning to locked session stays locked. SESSION ID METHOD: Use VS Code OTel JSONL export. Add 3 settings to templates/coding/.vscode/settings.json: github.copilot.chat.otel.enabled=true, github.copilot.chat.otel.exporterType=file, github.copilot.chat.otel.outfile pointing to .github/hooks/scripts/copilot-otel.jsonl. Parse the JSONL file to extract session.id (resource attribute) or gen_ai.conversation.id (span attribute). Fallback: if JSONL file missing or empty, generate a UUID4-based session ID with timestamp (graceful degradation). See docs/plans/vscode-session-id-methoden.md for full reference.
Goal: Security gate tracks denials per session, shows progressive Block N of M messaging, and locks sessions at the configured threshold. All 7 ACs of US-034 (1-7) are satisfied.
Comments: v3.2 Phase 2. Core counter implementation. Depends on DOC-010 for session ID strategy. SESSION ID: OTel JSONL method (Method 1 from vscode-session-id-methoden.md).

Name: Add counter configuration support
Description: Add configuration support for the denial counter: (1) Read lockout threshold (default 20) and enabled/disabled flag from a workspace configuration source (settings.json custom section or dedicated config file in .github/hooks/scripts/), (2) When counter is disabled via config flag, security gate skips all counter logic and never locks sessions, (3) When enabled, use the configured threshold M instead of hardcoded 20, (4) Ensure integrity hash is updated after config changes. The launcher GUI (GUI-019) will write these config values during project creation.
Goal: Counter threshold is configurable via workspace config file. Counter can be disabled entirely via config flag. ACs 9-10 of US-034 satisfied.
Comments: v3.2 Phase 2. Depends on SAF-035 (counter must exist before it can be configured).

If separate counters are not feasible, document the limitation.

Decision from plan: only log denied activities, (5) Propose integration point in security_gate.py, (6) Produce a design document with recommended approach.
Goal: A design document for deny-only audit logging with format specification, file location, and security_gate.py integration point.
Comments: v3.2 RESEARCH WP. Addresses open question Q7 from plan. Decision: log denied actions only.
